metadata.title: SYNGO_CC_5214
metadata.comment:
Rationale: Figure 4c: SHARPIN is enriched in PSD biochemical fractions from rat brain (similar to Shank).

Figure 5: reverse-IP in COS7 cells and rat cerebella showed that SHARPIN and SHANK1 can interact.

Figure 6, 8: immunofluorescence imaging showed SHARPIN and SHANK1 partially co-localized at excitatory postsynapses in cultured hippocampal neurons and cerebellum tissue.. Experimental description: M&M:
"Immunohistochemistry on Brain Sections and Cultured Neurons - Adult rats were perfused with 4% paraformaldehyde, and brain sections (50 mm) were cut using Vibratome after overnight postfixation."

antibody:
"To make Sharpin antibodies (1125, 9538, and 9716), the insert from the pGAD10 SR232 clone (aa 1-381 of Sharpin) was subcloned in frame into pRSETB (hexahistidine (H6) fusion protein vector; Invitrogen). Fusion proteins were purified by using Probond resin (Invitrogen) and used to immunize three rabbits (1125, 9538, and 9716). Sharpin-specific antibodies were purified from serum by using H6-Sharpin fusion proteins immobilized to PVDF membrane (Amersham Pharmacia Biotech). For double-immunofluorescence staining between Sharpin and Shank, we made a guinea pig Sharpin, a Novel Postsynaptic Density Protein 393 polyclonal Shank antibody (1123 and 1124) by using the GST fusion protein containing the SH3 1 PDZ domain (aa 469-691) of Shank1 as immunogen. Shank-specific antibodies were affinity-purified by using the Sulfolink column (Pierce) immobilized with thioredoxin fusion protein (pET32a; Novagen) containing the same insert as immunogen"
